naming_note: Shattered Reach maritime register throughout. Short names, functional surnames. Cross-checked against all previous regions. Halvaine at three-instance limit — no new Halvaines. Kern at two instances (Drath in Borderlands, Saltborn here) — one more possible but exercise caution.

pilot_station_staff.name: Vaela Stormcrest
pilot_station_staff.role: Senior pilot — Pilotage station, second to Halvard Warden
pilot_station_staff.physical: Has been piloting the Anchorage approaches for sixteen years. Knows the harbor as well as Halvard Warden and has been here almost as long.
pilot_station_staff.disposition: Professional, specifically good at reading vessels — their condition, their crew's state, their actual cargo versus their manifest cargo. Has been doing this for sixteen years and has developed the specific pattern-recognition that makes harbor pilots valuable.
pilot_station_staff.secret: Has noticed that the outer harbor ship's heading corrections are not random — they describe a consistent arc that she has plotted informally and that points south toward open ocean. Has mentioned this to Halvard Warden. He nodded and said he had noticed. Has been waiting to hear what he does with it.
pilot_station_staff.player_interaction: The pilot station work path's secondary contact. Her arc plot of the ship's course corrections is available to a player with maritime skills above 150 who earns her trust through competent harbor work.

pilot_station_staff.name: Drel Fenmark
pilot_station_staff.role: Junior pilot — Pilotage station, three years
pilot_station_staff.physical: Three years in and still learning what the harbor tells you if you let it.
pilot_station_staff.disposition: Enthusiastic about the work in the specific way of someone who chose this job and still finds it interesting.
pilot_station_staff.secret: Was the pilot who attempted the routine approach to the outer harbor ship on day two of its arrival. The captain refused the approach — claimed pilot services unnecessary for outer harbor anchorage, which is technically correct. The refusal itself was unusual. Drel filed a note on it and has been watching the ship since.
pilot_station_staff.player_interaction: His refusal-note is in the pilot station records. A player working at the pilot station who accesses records will find it.

compact_council_and_administration.name: Gorvan Tidefall
compact_council_and_administration.physical: Has been on the council for twenty-two years and in the chair for four. The weight of institutional responsibility in his manner — not unpleasant, simply present.
compact_council_and_administration.disposition: Cautious administrator. Has been managing the Iron Wake's presence and the Compact recognition negotiation simultaneously and has not noticed that the Iron Wake's actual reason for being present is not the negotiation.
compact_council_and_administration.secret: Has been told by two separate sources that something unusual is happening around the outer harbor ship. Has investigated neither because the Compact's administrative machinery moves at a specific pace and he has not yet determined this warrants accelerating it. This is a misjudgment he does not know he is making.
compact_council_and_administration.player_interaction: The formal Compact authority. Accessible through official channels. His misjudgment is visible to a player who has been paying attention — bringing him the full picture of the ship situation will produce administrative action faster than it would otherwise come.

compact_council_and_administration.name: Sela Bogward
compact_council_and_administration.note: No relation to Rhonda Bogward in the Free Fens
compact_council_and_administration.role: Customs clerk — flagging Halvwater notations for Wenda Saltcrest
compact_council_and_administration.physical: Has been in customs for seven years. Has developed the specific alertness of someone who processes three hundred manifests a week and has learned what wrong looks like.
compact_council_and_administration.disposition: Careful, follows Wenda Saltcrest's instructions precisely, does not ask why.
compact_council_and_administration.secret: Has flagged six Halvwater notations over three months. Does not know what she is helping build. Does not know Halvwater is a twenty-year embedding. Has been told only that certain notations are under review for administrative irregularity.
compact_council_and_administration.player_interaction: The merchants clerk spawn hook entry point. Her flagging behavior is the thread. A player who notices and follows it reaches Wenda Saltcrest.

compact_council_and_administration.name: Tav Coldwater
compact_council_and_administration.note: Coldwater appears in the Free Fens as Drev Coldwater — different person, different region, Coldwater is an acceptable common maritime/fen surname
compact_council_and_administration.role: Harbor administrator — day-to-day Compact harbor operations
compact_council_and_administration.physical: Has been managing the harbor's operational logistics for eleven years. The specific quality of someone who knows exactly how the harbor functions and what happens when it does not.
compact_council_and_administration.disposition: Efficient, not particularly curious about what he is not asked about.
compact_council_and_administration.secret: Processed the outer harbor ship's manifest discrepancy and was told by Halvwater to file it as a clerical error. Did so. Has been uncomfortable about it for three weeks. Has not elevated the discomfort because Halvwater is a council member and elevating discomfort about council members' instructions is not something the harbor administrator's role normally includes.
compact_council_and_administration.player_interaction: The clerical error notation thread from inside the administrative structure. His discomfort is visible. A player who approaches him about the notation will find someone who has been waiting for permission to be uncomfortable out loud.

tidewarden_cathedral_staff.name: Idda Corwatch
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.physical: Has been maintaining the cathedral library for eighteen years. Knows every volume.
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.disposition: Precise about access protocols, welcoming to genuine scholars, has a specific quality of alertness around the pre-Reckoning navigation chart that she cannot explain.
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.secret: Has been noticing that the pre-Reckoning chart with the submarine ridge notation has been accessed four times in the past month by different people — Cael Wyrnmark, a hedge scholar she does not recognize, someone who accessed it without signing the register (she only knows because the chart was in a slightly different position), and once by herself when she tried to understand why people kept looking at it. Has not mentioned this to the High Warden.
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.player_interaction: The hedge scholar spawn hook entry point. Her access log anomaly is available to a player who asks about the chart's recent use.

tidewarden_cathedral_staff.name: Brennan Tidemark
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.role: Junior Warden — Tidewarden Cathedral, pastoral duties
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.physical: Three years in pastoral service. Genuinely good at it in the specific way that some people are naturally suited to caring for others.
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.disposition: Warm, perceptive about people's emotional states, has been worried about the High Warden for three weeks.
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.secret: Has been following the High Warden to the burial site at night — at a distance, without her knowledge, out of concern. Has observed the burial site's quality of air change at night. Has experienced a mild version of what the High Warden experiences — not dramatic, a slight heightening of attention, a quality of the air being more present than usual. Has no framework for this and has been carrying it alone.
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.player_interaction: His nocturnal observation of the High Warden is a thread — he has information about the burial site's behavior at night that the High Warden herself has not fully articulated.

tidewarden_cathedral_staff.name: Brother Kern Holmswick
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.note: No relation to the Free Fens Elder Wren Holmswick
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.physical: Has been in the cathedral for thirty years. Has an institutional memory that the High Warden respects and occasionally uses.
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.disposition: Observant in the specific way of someone who has been in the same building for thirty years and notices everything that changes.
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.secret: Has noticed that Oswin Halvwater attends services regularly and never takes communion. Has been noting this for nine years. Has mentioned it to no one because it is not his place to note the devotional practices of Compact council members. Is waiting for it to be relevant to something. Has a feeling it is becoming relevant.
tidewarden_cathedral_staff.player_interaction: His nine-year observation of Halvwater's communion avoidance is one thread in the embedding pattern. Available to a player investigating Halvwater through Church records or community observation.

iron_wake_crew.name: Sorn Waveward
iron_wake_crew.physical: Has been the Iron Wake's first mate for six years. Carries this with the specific quality of someone who has learned to be exceptionally competent in close proximity to an exceptionally capable leader without being diminished by the comparison.
iron_wake_crew.disposition: Professional extension of the Iron Wake's will. Not a replacement, not a rival — a practical amplification.
iron_wake_crew.secret: Has been conducting the disciplined harbor walk surveillance passes. Has identified the cult's night boat approaches before the Iron Wake reviewed his reports. Has also identified, through a different observation, that Halvard Warden is more than a harbor pilot — the quality of attention is visible to someone who has spent six years in the Corsair Throne's intelligence-adjacent operations.
iron_wake_crew.player_interaction: The deserter spawn hook contact. His recruitment offer represents the Iron Wake's interest in the outer harbor ship. Following this thread leads directly to the Iron Wake's operational planning.

iron_wake_crew.name: Essa Saltmark
iron_wake_crew.role: Intelligence officer — Corsair Throne, Iron Wake's vessel
iron_wake_crew.physical: The specific practiced unremarkability of someone who has been doing intelligence work in port environments since her mid-twenties.
iron_wake_crew.disposition: Has developed the precise habit of recording observations before analyzing them — a discipline that produces comprehensive intelligence and occasionally produces things she wishes she had not seen clearly.
iron_wake_crew.secret: Has been compiling a dossier on the outer harbor ship's manifest discrepancy from customs records she accessed through an unofficial channel. The dossier includes the names of the two additional crew members — Kern Drath and Sela Voss — cross-referenced against Varathi Return cult member sightings in Keth from a different intelligence source. The connection between the Keth closure and the Anchorage ship is in her dossier. Has not yet given the Iron Wake the full dossier because she is still verifying the Keth connection.
iron_wake_crew.player_interaction: Her dossier's Keth connection is the most significant piece of intelligence about the ship available in the Anchorage. A player who finds her and demonstrates knowledge of the Keth situation will accelerate her verification and receive a direct briefing.

harbor_community.name: Wren Daltborn
harbor_community.role: Dock worker — outer harbor berthing operations
harbor_community.physical: Has been working the outer harbor berthing for eight years. Has the specific quality of someone who has been offered bribes before and has developed a clear policy about them.
harbor_community.disposition: Not particularly principled about many things. Specifically principled about bribes because he has seen what happens to dock workers who take them and has decided the money is not worth it.
harbor_community.secret: Was approached by a cult operative with an offer for access to the outer harbor berthing records. Refused. Has been talking about it to his regular group at The Tide's End inn. Three people in his regular group have now heard the story. The information is actively spreading through the Outer Harbor working community.
harbor_community.player_interaction: The most accessible thread to the cult's operational mistakes. His story is available at The Tide's End to any player who spends time in the Outer Harbor working community.

harbor_community.name: Tev Roughwater
harbor_community.role: Former harbor pilot — retired, The Tide's End regular
harbor_community.physical: Retired from piloting seven years ago. Still spends most of his time at the harbor because he does not know where else to be.
harbor_community.disposition: Has the specific knowledge of thirty-two years of harbor piloting and uses it to make observations that the current generation of pilots then have to decide whether to act on.
harbor_community.secret: Has been watching the outer harbor ship for three weeks from the Tide's End's harbor-facing window. Has formed a specific opinion about the course correction pattern — he calls it 'the current calling,' which is his experiential terminology for a vessel that is responding to something in the water that the crew cannot explain. Has seen it twice in thirty-two years. Both previous instances were related to unusual deep-water activity.
harbor_community.player_interaction: His 'current calling' observation and his two previous instances are available over drinks. Both previous instances are recorded in the Anchorage's pilot station historical records if a player knows to look.

harbor_community.name: Fen Daltwick
harbor_community.physical: Has been provisioning ships in the Outer Harbor for fifteen years. Has the specific knowledge of someone who knows exactly what crews need and what their current stock levels say about how long they have been in harbor.
harbor_community.disposition: Commercial, attentive, specifically attentive to the outer harbor ship because it has been his most regular customer for three weeks.
harbor_community.secret: Has been provisioning the Pale Current every five days. The captain always receives him aboard personally. The two specific crew members who come up for provisioning look wrong — not sick-wrong, orientation-wrong. They keep moving toward the ship's southern rail when on deck. Has not mentioned this to anyone because it is not his business and he is being paid.
harbor_community.player_interaction: The provisioning schedule and the two crew members' behavior are available to a player who approaches him through legitimate chandler business. His five-day provisioning visits are the only regular contact with the ship's deck that the harbor community has.

harbor_community.name: Brynn Wavefall
harbor_community.role: Harbor diver — maintenance, salvage, marine biology enthusiast
harbor_community.physical: The specific build of someone who spends significant time in cold water.
harbor_community.disposition: Genuinely interested in the harbor's underwater environment in a way that has produced unusual practical knowledge.
harbor_community.secret: Has been diving near the outer harbor ship for her own research purposes and has noticed that the bioluminescence directly beneath the ship is behaving differently than the surrounding harbor — more intense, with a directional quality, responding to something in or on the ship rather than just to movement. Has shared this with two people. Neither understood what she was describing.
harbor_community.player_interaction: Her observation of the bioluminescence behavior beneath the ship is one more signal of the disc's presence — the same cold-to-touch quality that the containment markers produce is apparently causing a response in the harbor's bioluminescent organisms at close range. A player with Natural Philosophy above 150 who hears her description will recognize this as consistent with the artifact effect.

harbor_community.name: Gorvan Saltholm
harbor_community.role: Fishing community elder — The Shelf, eastern island
harbor_community.physical: Has been fishing the Anchorage waters for forty-five years. Knows the harbor's underwater environment through the behavior of what lives in it.
harbor_community.disposition: The specific patience of someone who has been reading the same water for four decades and finds other people's readings interesting primarily when they confirm or contradict his own.
harbor_community.secret: Has noticed that the fish beneath the outer harbor ship are not behaving normally. Has attributed this to the bioluminescence disturbance but has also noted that the disturbance direction is consistent — it points south. Forty-five years of reading what fish do tells him something is wrong about the ship in a way that is not about the ship.
harbor_community.player_interaction: His fish-behavior observation is a confirmation thread. Available to players who spend time on The Shelf.

general_anchorage_population.name: Mira Daltcrest
general_anchorage_population.physical: Has been operating in the lower tier market for twelve years. The specific quality of someone who sells things that do not have physical form.
general_anchorage_population.disposition: Commercially neutral about the content of what she sells. Has her own moral limits but they are not where most people expect them.
general_anchorage_population.secret: Has been approached by three separate parties in the past week about the outer harbor ship — the cult (through Thornwick's operation, who did not identify themselves as cult), a Compact administrative official, and the Iron Wake's intelligence officer. Has sold each of them different and non-overlapping information. Has noted that all three are asking about the same ship from different directions.
general_anchorage_population.player_interaction: The petty criminal background's natural contact. Her three-direction observation is available to a player who approaches her correctly — she will note that she has sold information about the outer harbor ship to three separate parties and that this suggests the ship's situation is more significant than its manifest implies.

general_anchorage_population.name: Sela Fenrath
general_anchorage_population.role: Varathi Return cult operative — Thornwick's Anchorage operation
general_anchorage_population.physical: Has been in the Anchorage for two weeks. Does not know the Anchorage. Is making mistakes.
general_anchorage_population.disposition: Committed to the cult's work, specifically not equipped for harbor operational security.
general_anchorage_population.secret: Was the operative who approached the dock worker Wren Daltborn with the bribe offer. Has not told Thornwick that the dock worker refused and is talking about it. Has been hoping the information stops spreading before Thornwick finds out.
general_anchorage_population.player_interaction: One of Halvard Warden's catalogued mistakes in embodied form. A player who follows the dock worker bribe thread will find Sela Fenrath as the thread's origin.

general_anchorage_population.name: Rael Tidefall
general_anchorage_population.note: No relation to Compact council chair Gorvan Tidefall. Tidefall is a common Shattered Reach surname.
general_anchorage_population.role: Varathi Return cult operative — Thornwick's Anchorage operation
general_anchorage_population.physical: The more competent of the two operatives. Has been making fewer mistakes but has not corrected the ones already made.
general_anchorage_population.disposition: Is aware that the operation is not going well. Has been considering whether to tell Thornwick. Has decided that telling Thornwick requires explaining why they know things are not going well, which requires explaining what they have observed, which reveals observations they should not have needed to make if the operation had been properly planned.
general_anchorage_population.secret: Has identified Kern Saltborn as something more than a retired chandler. Has not been able to determine what. Has been watching Saltborn watch them and has correctly concluded that the situation is worse than Thornwick knows.
general_anchorage_population.player_interaction: The more dangerous of the two operatives specifically because they are aware something is wrong. A player who intercepts their communication to Thornwick about Saltborn will find a cult operation that is about to make a significant decision under incomplete information.

general_anchorage_population.name: Idwal Bogfen
general_anchorage_population.role: The person Sova Fenwick moved out of the northwest fen — arrived in the Anchorage instead of the western staging point
general_anchorage_population.physical: Shows contamination exposure symptoms — not acute, the moderate presentation of someone who was exposed to the northwest contamination zone for several days before being moved. The directional awareness is ongoing and in the Anchorage, where open ocean is visible, has intensified.
general_anchorage_population.disposition: Frightened. Has been in the Anchorage for two weeks. Has not sought medical attention because they do not know who to trust. Has been orienting toward the harbor's southern exposure compulsively.
general_anchorage_population.secret: Their directional awareness points south — the same heading as the two affected crew members on the outer harbor ship. They came from the northwest fen. The crew members came from the Keth plateau. The same heading from different geographic starting points that are thousands of miles apart.
general_anchorage_population.the_connection: Idwal Bogfen and the two crew members Kern Drath and Sela Voss are all experiencing the same directional phenomenon from different exposure sources — the northwest fen's degrading sealing and the Keth disc/inscription respectively. A player who encounters all three and recognizes the shared heading has assembled one of the most significant pieces of cross-regional intelligence available in the game: whatever is at that southern ocean location is the destination that multiple separate artifact systems are pointing toward.
general_anchorage_population.player_interaction: The hollow road runner spawn hook reveals that the person Sova moved arrived in the Anchorage. Finding Idwal requires knowing to look — they are staying in a cheap room in the lower tier and rarely come out except to stand at the harbor's southern-facing seawall and look at the water. A player with medical skills who encounters them will find a frightened young person who can be helped and who is an extraordinary piece of cross-regional intelligence.

general_anchorage_population.name: Edda Waveborn
general_anchorage_population.note: Edda appears in the Free Fens as Edda Vrane — different region, different person, Edda is at the two-instance limit. No more Eddas.
general_anchorage_population.role: Salt Silence contact — attends cathedral services, information node
general_anchorage_population.physical: The specific unremarkability of someone who has been doing this for fifteen years.
general_anchorage_population.disposition: Has developed the patience of someone who attends every Tidewarden service not from faith but from the information available in a congregation that includes representatives of every Anchorage power.
general_anchorage_population.secret: Has been reporting Oswin Halvwater's communion avoidance to Kern Saltborn for nine years. The first eight years this was filed as a low-significance behavioral note. The ninth year — when Halvwater filed the clerical error notation — Saltborn pulled the nine-year file and made it significant.
general_anchorage_population.player_interaction: The nine-year communion file is a thread available through Kern Saltborn. A player building a case against Halvwater who reaches Saltborn will receive this as part of the picture.

harbor_services.name: Kern Ashstorm
harbor_services.role: Pilot boat crew — works with Halvard Warden
harbor_services.physical: Has been on the pilot boats for one year. Still learning.
harbor_services.disposition: The enthusiasm of someone new to a job they chose.
harbor_services.secret: Has been assigned to the outer harbor rounds by Halvard Warden three times this week rather than the usual one. Has noticed that Warden spends more time looking at the outer harbor ship than the rounds require. Has not asked why because Warden does not invite questions about where he looks.
harbor_services.player_interaction: His awareness of Warden's specific attention to the outer harbor ship is a minor confirming thread.

harbor_services.name: Drev Shorefall
harbor_services.role: Innkeeper — The Tide's End, Outer Harbor
harbor_services.physical: Has been running the Outer Harbor's roughest inn for fourteen years. The specific quality of someone who has seen everything and has concluded that most of it is less remarkable than it appears.
harbor_services.disposition: Neutral hospitality. Has an iron rule: no violence in the common room.
harbor_services.secret: Has been watching Dava Salthorn eat alone at the fourth table for three weeks and has formed an accurate assessment of her situation — captain of the outer harbor ship, hired cargo she did not fully vet, waiting for a contact who has not come. Has not spoken to her about this assessment because he considers it her business and not his.
harbor_services.player_interaction: His accurate assessment of Dava Salthorn is available to a player who has been in The Tide's End long enough for him to trust them with an observation. It would confirm that Dava Salthorn is the right approach to the ship.

harbor_services.name: Gorvan Fenwick
harbor_services.note: Gorvan appears twice previously (Keth council Gorvan Arvane, Amber Coast Gorvan Delvane) — three instances, at the limit. No more Gorvans.
harbor_services.role: Innkeeper — The Silver Tide, Cathedral Quarter
harbor_services.physical: Has been running the Anchorage's finest inn for twenty years. The specific quality of someone who has been managing powerful people's need for discretion for two decades.
harbor_services.disposition: Discretion as a professional virtue so deeply held it reads as his natural character.
harbor_services.secret: Rael Sorveth has been staying at the Silver Tide for six days. He knows who she is. He has told no one. He considers this a standard professional obligation. He has also noticed that she came to the common room last evening for specific reasons and that three other people in the common room noticed her notice them, and that this structure of observing and being observed is something he has seen in the Silver Tide common room before and that it always precedes something.
harbor_services.player_interaction: His awareness that the Iron Wake's common room visit was strategic is available to a player who has been at the Silver Tide long enough to be trusted with an observation. The innkeeper's child spawn hook entry point.
